name: H-1B
tagline: The most common path for skilled professionals to work in the United States
description: The H-1B visa allows U.S. employers to temporarily employ foreign workers in specialty occupation roles that require at least a bachelor's degree or equivalent. It is the primary visa category for tech workers, engineers, scientists, financial analysts, architects, and other highly skilled professionals.

requirements: Position must qualify as a 'specialty occupation' requiring at least a bachelor's degree in a specific field
Applicant must hold a U.S. bachelor's degree or foreign equivalent in the relevant field
A U.S. employer must sponsor the petition — self-employment is not permitted
Employer must file a Labor Condition Application (LCA) with the Department of Labor
Subject to an annual cap of 65,000 visas (plus 20,000 for U.S. master's degree holders)
Cap-exempt employers (universities, nonprofits affiliated with universities, government research organizations) can file year-round
benefits: Valid for 3 years, extendable to 6 years
Can be extended beyond 6 years if a green card application is pending
Allows dual intent — you can pursue permanent residency while on H-1B
Spouse and children under 21 may accompany on H-4 visa
H-4 EAD allows spouses to work in many circumstances
Can transfer to a new employer without leaving the U.S.

steps.title: Employer files LCA
steps.description: Labor Condition Application submitted to Department of Labor. Typically takes 7 business days.

steps.title: Lottery registration
steps.description: For cap-subject petitions, USCIS conducts a lottery in March each year. Registration window is typically March 1–18.

steps.title: Petition filing
steps.description: If selected in lottery, employer files Form I-129 with supporting documents. Premium processing available for 15-business-day adjudication.

steps.title: Approval & start date
steps.description: H-1B cap-subject employment can begin October 1 of the fiscal year.

processing.timeline: 3–6 months standard; 15 business days with premium processing
processing.cost: Government fees range from $1,710–$6,460 depending on employer size and options. Attorney fees additional.
documents: Signed job offer letter with job description and salary
Employer documentation (EIN, company description, financial records)
If applicable: credential evaluation for foreign degrees

faqs.question: Can a startup or small company sponsor an H-1B?
faqs.answer: Yes. Even early-stage startups with few or no employees can sponsor an H-1B visa. The key requirements are that the company is a legitimate U.S. employer, there is a valid employer-employee relationship, and the position qualifies as a specialty occupation. We regularly help startups navigate this process.

faqs.question: What happens if I'm not selected in the H-1B lottery?
faqs.answer: If you're not selected, there are several alternatives: O-1 visa for extraordinary ability (no lottery, no employer cap), L-1 for intracompany transfers, TN for Canadian and Mexican nationals, cap-exempt H-1B through a qualifying nonprofit or university, or pursuing an EB-2 National Interest Waiver if you qualify. We offer free webinars each year on post-lottery options.

faqs.question: How does H-1B transfer work when changing jobs?
faqs.answer: H-1B portability allows you to start working for a new employer as soon as the new employer files an H-1B transfer petition — you don't have to wait for approval. The new employer must be a legitimate U.S. employer and the role must qualify as a specialty occupation.

faqs.question: Can my spouse work on an H-4 visa?
faqs.answer: H-4 visa holders whose spouse has an approved I-140 immigrant petition (the first step toward a green card) may apply for H-4 EAD (Employment Authorization Document), which allows unrestricted work authorization. This is currently under regulatory review, so we recommend consulting with us for the latest rules.

faqs.question: What is cap-exempt H-1B and who qualifies?
faqs.answer: Cap-exempt H-1B petitions are not subject to the annual 65,000 lottery cap. They can be filed at any time of year. Qualifying employers include universities, nonprofit research organizations, and government research entities. If you work at or have a job offer from such an employer, you may file immediately without waiting for the lottery.

faqs.question: How long can I stay on H-1B status?
faqs.answer: The initial H-1B is granted for 3 years and can be extended for another 3 years, for a total of 6 years. If you have an approved I-140 petition or a pending labor certification that has been filed for more than 365 days, you may be eligible for 1-year extensions beyond the 6-year limit until your green card case is resolved.
